author: audit-agent

patch.value: During sandbox extension lifecycle audit, pm extension doctor reported extension_load_failed for local installs because Node could not resolve @unbrained/pm-cli from extension directory. Need deterministic UX: either improve install/runtime behavior or strengthen diagnostics/docs to make dependency expectations explicit.
Clarify or harden SDK import resolution for local extension installs
Dogfood extension install from local path failed to load when extension imported @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk without its own dependency install.
Local extension install path provides deterministic guidance (or runtime support) for SDK import resolution and doctor output points directly to remediation.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'audit extension sdk calendar docs' --mode keyword --limit 10; pm list-open --fields id,title,type,parent,status,priority --limit 50; pm list-in-progress --fields id,title,type,parent,status,priority --limit 50. Net-new because active items cover extension runtime probe/docs parity but not local install SDK module-resolution behavior.

message: create issue for local extension sdk resolution gap

value.note: module load and import resolution path

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Repro evidence from sandbox audit: local extension install succeeds, but doctor reports extension_load_failed when extension entry imports @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk and source package has no installed dependency. Runtime error: Cannot find package '@unbrained/pm-cli' imported from installed extension index.js.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: Potential remediation paths: (a) install-time dependency validation/warning for local sources, (b) extension loader resolution fallback to pm CLI SDK for explicit compatibility mode, or (c) tighter doctor remediation hints with concrete npm install guidance.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Follow-up evidence from 2026-04-26 audit: local extension imports of @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk require ESM package metadata type module in addition to dependency installation. Without type module, extension install appears successful but runtime command path remains unavailable with only a Node module warning. Recommend install-time or doctor diagnostics that explicitly call out missing ESM package type alongside dependency guidance.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Latest sandbox run confirms @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk imports work when extension package metadata and module setup are correct. Remaining ergonomics gap is actionable diagnostics when extension appears installed but runtime command remains unavailable due packaging mismatch; doctor guidance can be more direct.
